questions.questionText: The number of ways five alphabets can be chosen from the alphabets of the word MATHEMATICS, where the chosen alphabets are not necessarily distinct, is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$A=\{2,3,6,8,9,11\}$$ and $$B=\{1,4,5,10,15\}$$. Let $$R$$ be a relation on $$A \times B$$ defined by $$(a, b) R(c, d)$$ if and only if $$3 a d-7 b c$$ is an even integer. Then the relation $$R$$ is
options:
(A) reflexive but not symmetric.
(B) an equivalence relation.
(C) reflexive and symmetric but not transitive.
(D) transitive but not symmetric.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\int_\limits\alpha^{\log _e 4} \frac{\mathrm{d} x}{\sqrt{\mathrm{e}^x-1}}=\frac{\pi}{6}$$. Then $$\mathrm{e}^\alpha$$ and $$\mathrm{e}^{-\alpha}$$ are the roots of the equation :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=4 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}, \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=11 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}$$ and $$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}$$ be a vector such that $$(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}) \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \times(-2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+3 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}})$$. If $$(2 \vec{a}+3 \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c}=1670$$, then $$|\vec{c}|^2$$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the term independent of $$x$$ in the expansion of $$\left(\sqrt{\mathrm{a}} x^2+\frac{1}{2 x^3}\right)^{10}$$ is 105 , then $$\mathrm{a}^2$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the function $$f(x)=2 x^3-9 \mathrm{ax}^2+12 \mathrm{a}^2 x+1, \mathrm{a}> 0$$ has a local maximum at $$x=\alpha$$ and a local minimum at $$x=\alpha^2$$, then $$\alpha$$ and $$\alpha^2$$ are the roots of the equation :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}, \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}$$ and $$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}=3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\lambda \hat{k}$$ be three vectors. Let $$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}$$ be a unit vector along $$\vec{b}+\vec{c}$$. If $$\vec{r} \cdot \vec{a}=3$$, then $$3 \lambda$$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: For $$\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}>0$$, let $$f(x)= \begin{cases}\frac{\tan ((\mathrm{a}+1) x)+\mathrm{b} \tan x}{x}, & x< 0 \\ 3, & x=0 \\ \frac{\sqrt{\mathrm{a} x+\mathrm{b}^2 x^2}-\sqrt{\mathrm{a} x}}{\mathrm{~b} \sqrt{\mathrm{a}} x \sqrt{x}}, & x> 0\end{cases}$$ be a continuous function at $$x=0$$. Then $$\frac{\mathrm{b}}{\mathrm{a}}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the image of the point $$(-4,5)$$ in the line $$x+2 y=2$$ lies on the circle $$(x+4)^2+(y-3)^2=r^2$$, then $$r$$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: In an increasing geometric progression of positive terms, the sum of the second and sixth terms is $$\frac{70}{3}$$ and the product of the third and fifth terms is 49. Then the sum of the $$4^{\text {th }}, 6^{\text {th }}$$ and $$8^{\text {th }}$$ terms is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The sum of all possible values of $$\theta \in[-\pi, 2 \pi]$$, for which $$\frac{1+i \cos \theta}{1-2 i \cos \theta}$$ is purely imaginary, is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the shortest distance between the lines $$\frac{x-\lambda}{2}=\frac{y-4}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}$$ and $$\frac{x-2}{4}=\frac{y-4}{6}=\frac{z-7}{8}$$ is $$\frac{13}{\sqrt{29}}$$, then a value of $$\lambda$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$y=y(x)$$ be the solution curve of the differential equation $$\sec y \frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{~d} x}+2 x \sin y=x^3 \cos y, y(1)=0$$. Then $$y(\sqrt{3})$$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: There are three bags $$X, Y$$ and $$Z$$. Bag $$X$$ contains 5 one-rupee coins and 4 five-rupee coins; Bag $$Y$$ contains 4 one-rupee coins and 5 five-rupee coins and Bag $$Z$$ contains 3 one-rupee coins and 6 five-rupee coins. A bag is selected at random and a coin drawn from it at random is found to be a one-rupee coin. Then the probability, that it came from bag $$\mathrm{Y}$$, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the line segment joining the points $$(5,2)$$ and $$(2, a)$$ subtends an angle $$\frac{\pi}{4}$$ at the origin, then the absolute value of the product of all possible values of $a$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $$\alpha eq \mathrm{a}, eta eq \mathrm{b}, \gamma eq \mathrm{c}$$ and $$\left| egin{array}{lll}\alpha & \mathrm{b} & \mathrm{c} \ \mathrm{a} & eta & \mathrm{c} \ \mathrm{a} & \mathrm{b} & \gamma\end{array} ight|=0$$, then $$ rac{\mathrm{a}}{\alpha-\mathrm{a}}+ rac{\mathrm{b}}{ eta-\mathrm{b}}+ rac{\gamma}{\gamma-\mathrm{c}}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ccc}-\mathrm{a} & \text { if } & -\mathrm{a} \leq x \leq 0 \\ x+\mathrm{a} & \text { if } & 0< x \leq \mathrm{a}\end{array}\right.$$ where $$\mathrm{a}> 0$$ and $$\mathrm{g}(x)=(f(|x|)-|f(x)|) / 2$$. Then the function $$g:[-a, a] \rightarrow[-a, a]$$ is
options:
(A) neither one-one nor onto.
(B) both one-one and onto.
(C) one-one.
(D) onto
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $$\int \frac{1}{\sqrt[5]{(x-1)^4(x+3)^6}} \mathrm{~d} x=\mathrm{A}\left(\frac{\alpha x-1}{\beta x+3}\right)^B+\mathrm{C}$$, where $$\mathrm{C}$$ is the constant of integration, then the value of $$\alpha+\beta+20 \mathrm{AB}$$ is _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the system of equations $$x+4 y-z=\lambda, 7 x+9 y+\mu z=-3,5 x+y+2 z=-1$$ has infinitely many solutions, then $$(2 \mu+3 \lambda)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
An arithmetic progression is written in the following way

The sum of all the terms of the 10th row is _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The area of the region in the first quadrant inside the circle $$x^2+y^2=8$$ and outside the parabola $$y^2=2 x$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the value of $$\frac{3 \cos 36^{\circ}+5 \sin 18^{\circ}}{5 \cos 36^{\circ}-3 \sin 18^{\circ}}$$ is $$\frac{a \sqrt{5}-b}{c}$$, where $$a, b, c$$ are natural numbers and $$\operatorname{gcd}(a, c)=1$$, then $$a+b+c$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{P}(\alpha, \beta, \gamma)$$ be the image of the point $$\mathrm{Q}(1,6,4)$$ in the line $$\frac{x}{1}=\frac{y-1}{2}=\frac{z-2}{3}$$. Then $$2 \alpha+\beta+\gamma$$ is equal to ________
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{A}$$ be the region enclosed by the parabola $$y^2=2 x$$ and the line $$x=24$$. Then the maximum area of the rectangle inscribed in the region $$\mathrm{A}$$ is ________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{S}$$ be the focus of the hyperbola $$\frac{x^2}{3}-\frac{y^2}{5}=1$$, on the positive $$x$$-axis. Let $$\mathrm{C}$$ be the circle with its centre at $$\mathrm{A}(\sqrt{6}, \sqrt{5})$$ and passing through the point $$\mathrm{S}$$. If $$\mathrm{O}$$ is the origin and $$\mathrm{SAB}$$ is a diameter of $$\mathrm{C}$$, then the square of the area of the triangle OSB is equal to __________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The number of distinct real roots of the equation $$|x+1||x+3|-4|x+2|+5=0$$, is _______
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let a ray of light passing through the point $$(3,10)$$ reflects on the line $$2 x+y=6$$ and the reflected ray passes through the point $$(7,2)$$. If the equation of the incident ray is $$a x+b y+1=0$$, then $$a^2+b^2+3 a b$$ is equal to _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\alpha|x|=|y| \mathrm{e}^{x y-\beta}, \alpha, \beta \in \mathbf{N}$$ be the solution of the differential equation $$x \mathrm{~d} y-y \mathrm{~d} x+x y(x \mathrm{~d} y+y \mathrm{~d} x)=0,y(1)=2$$. Then $$\alpha+\beta$$ is equal to ________
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $$\alpha=\lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 0^{+}}\left(\frac{\mathrm{e}^{\sqrt{\tan x}}-\mathrm{e}^{\sqrt{x}}}{\sqrt{\tan x}-\sqrt{x}}\right)$$ and $$\beta=\lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 0}(1+\sin x)^{\frac{1}{2} \cot x}$$ are the roots of the quadratic equation $$\mathrm{a} x^2+\mathrm{b} x-\sqrt{\mathrm{e}}=0$$, then $$12 \log _{\mathrm{e}}(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b})$$ is equal to _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The position of the image formed by the combination of lenses is :
options:
(A) $$15 \mathrm{~cm}$$ (right of second lens)
(B) $$30 \mathrm{~cm}$$ (right of third lens)
(C) $$30 \mathrm{~cm}$$ (left of third lens)
(D) $$15 \mathrm{~cm}$$ (left of second lens)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c} \in \mathbf{N}$$ and $$\mathrm{a}< \mathrm{b}< \mathrm{c}$$. Let the mean, the mean deviation about the mean and the variance of the 5 observations $$9,25, a, b, c$$ be 18, 4 and $$\frac{136}{5}$$, respectively. Then $$2 a+b-c$$ is equal to ________
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A cube of ice floats partly in water and partly in kerosene oil. The ratio of volume of ice immersed in water to that in kerosene oil (specific gravity of Kerosene oil = 0.8, specific gravity of ice = 0.9):
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A block is simply released from the top of an inclined plane as shown in the figure above. The maximum compression in the spring when the block hits the spring is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
In a hypothetical fission reaction

$${ }_{92} X^{236} \rightarrow{ }_{56} \mathrm{Y}^{141}+{ }_{36} Z^{92}+3 R$$

The identity of emitted particles (R) is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A thin circular disc of mass $$\mathrm{M}$$ and radius $$\mathrm{R}$$ is rotating in a horizontal plane about an axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to its plane with angular velocity $$\omega$$. If another disc of same dimensions but of mass $$\mathrm{M} / 2$$ is placed gently on the first disc co-axially, then the new angular velocity of the system is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A proton and an electron have the same de Broglie wavelength. If $$\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}$$ and $$\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{e}}$$ be the kinetic energies of proton and electron respectively, then choose the correct relation :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If $$M_0$$ is the mass of isotope $${ }_5^{12} B, M_p$$ and $$M_n$$ are the masses of proton and neutron, then nuclear binding energy of isotope is:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A capacitor has air as dielectric medium and two conducting plates of area $$12 \mathrm{~cm}^2$$ and they are $$0.6 \mathrm{~cm}$$ apart. When a slab of dielectric having area $$12 \mathrm{~cm}^2$$ and $$0.6 \mathrm{~cm}$$ thickness is inserted between the plates, one of the conducting plates has to be moved by $$0.2 \mathrm{~cm}$$ to keep the capacitance same as in previous case. The dielectric constant of the slab is : (Given $$\epsilon_0=8.834 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{~F} / \mathrm{m}$$)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two satellite A and B go round a planet in circular orbits having radii 4R and R respectively. If the speed of $$\mathrm{A}$$ is $$3 v$$, the speed of $$\mathrm{B}$$ will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Least count of a vernier caliper is $$\frac{1}{20 \mathrm{~N}} \mathrm{~cm}$$. The value of one division on the main scale is $$1 \mathrm{~mm}$$. Then the number of divisions of main scale that coincide with $$\mathrm{N}$$ divisions of vernier scale is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The angle of projection for a projectile to have same horizontal range and maximum height is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A diatomic gas $$(\gamma=1.4)$$ does $$100 \mathrm{~J}$$ of work in an isobaric expansion. The heat given to the gas is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: There are 100 divisions on the circular scale of a screw gauge of pitch $$1 \mathrm{~mm}$$. With no measuring quantity in between the jaws, the zero of the circular scale lies 5 divisions below the reference line. The diameter of a wire is then measured using this screw gauge. It is found that 4 linear scale divisions are clearly visible while 60 divisions on circular scale coincide with the reference line. The diameter of the wire is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A coil of negligible resistance is connected in series with $$90 \Omega$$ resistor across $$120 \mathrm{~V}, 60 \mathrm{~Hz}$$ supply. A voltmeter reads $$36 \mathrm{~V}$$ across resistance. Inductance of the coil is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement (I) : The mean free path of gas molecules is inversely proportional to square of molecular diameter.

Statement (II) : Average kinetic energy of gas molecules is directly proportional to absolute temperature of gas.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
(C) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
(D) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A given object takes $$\mathrm{n}$$ times the time to slide down $$45^{\circ}$$ rough inclined plane as it takes the time to slide down an identical perfectly smooth $$45^{\circ}$$ inclined plane. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the object and the surface of inclined plane is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A plane progressive wave is given by $$y=2 \cos 2 \pi(330 \mathrm{t}-x) \mathrm{m}$$. The frequency of the wave is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Water boils in an electric kettle in 20 minutes after being switched on. Using the same main supply, the length of the heating element should be _________ to __________ times of its initial length if the water is to be boiled in 15 minutes.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If $$\epsilon_{\mathrm{o}}$$ is the permittivity of free space and $$\mathrm{E}$$ is the electric field, then $$\epsilon_{\mathrm{o}} \mathrm{E}^2$$ has the dimensions :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A heater is designed to operate with a power of $$1000 \mathrm{~W}$$ in a $$100 \mathrm{~V}$$ line. It is connected in combination with a resistance of $$10 \Omega$$ and a resistance $$R$$, to a $$100 \mathrm{~V}$$ mains as shown in figure. For the heater to operate at $$62.5 \mathrm{~W}$$, the value of $$\mathrm{R}$$ should be _______ $$\Omega$$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A long straight wire of radius a carries a steady current I. The current is uniformly distributed across its cross section. The ratio of the magnetic field at $$\frac{a}{2}$$ and $$2 a$$ from axis of the wire is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A body of mass M thrown horizontally with velocity v from the top of the tower of height H touches the ground at a distance of $$100 \mathrm{~m}$$ from the foot of the tower. A body of mass $$2 \mathrm{~M}$$ thrown at a velocity $$\frac{v}{2}$$ from the top of the tower of height $$4 \mathrm{H}$$ will touch the ground at a distance of _______ m.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The coercivity of a magnet is $$5 \times 10^3 \mathrm{~A} / \mathrm{m}$$. The amount of current required to be passed in a solenoid of length $$30 \mathrm{~cm}$$ and the number of turns 150, so that the magnet gets demagnetised when inside the solenoid is ________ A.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A potential divider circuit is connected with a dc source of $$20 \mathrm{~V}$$, a light emitting diode of glow in voltage $$1.8 \mathrm{~V}$$ and a zener diode of breakdown voltage of $$3.2 \mathrm{~V}$$. The length (PR) of the resistive wire is $$20 \mathrm{~cm}$$. The minimum length of PQ to just glow the LED is _________ $$\mathrm{cm}$$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A circular table is rotating with an angular velocity of $$\omega \mathrm{~rad} / \mathrm{s}$$ about its axis (see figure). There is a smooth groove along a radial direction on the table. A steel ball is gently placed at a distance of $$1 \mathrm{~m}$$ on the groove. All the surfaces are smooth. If the radius of the table is $$3 \mathrm{~m}$$, the radial velocity of the ball w.r.t. the table at the time ball leaves the table is $$x \sqrt{2} \omega \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$$, where the value of $$x$$ is _________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two slits are $$1 \mathrm{~mm}$$ apart and the screen is located $$1 \mathrm{~m}$$ away from the slits. A light of wavelength $$500 \mathrm{~nm}$$ is used. The width of each slit to obtain 10 maxima of the double slit pattern within the central maximum of the single slit pattern is __________ $$\times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~m}$$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If the net electric field at point $$\mathrm{P}$$ along $$\mathrm{Y}$$ axis is zero, then the ratio of $$\left|\frac{q_2}{q_3}\right|$$ is $$\frac{8}{5 \sqrt{x}}$$, where $$x=$$ ________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An object of mass $$0.2 \mathrm{~kg}$$ executes simple harmonic motion along $$x$$ axis with frequency of $$\left(\frac{25}{\pi}\right) \mathrm{Hz}$$. At the position $$x=0.04 \mathrm{~m}$$ the object has kinetic energy $$0.5 \mathrm{~J}$$ and potential energy $$0.4 \mathrm{~J}$$. The amplitude of oscillation is ________ $$\mathrm{cm}$$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Small water droplets of radius $$0.01 \mathrm{~mm}$$ are formed in the upper atmosphere and falling with a terminal velocity of $$10 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}$$. Due to condensation, if 8 such droplets are coalesced and formed a larger drop, the new terminal velocity will be ________ $$\mathrm{cm} / \mathrm{s}$$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An alternating emf $$\mathrm{E}=110 \sqrt{2} \sin 100 \mathrm{t}$$ volt is applied to a capacitor of $$2 \mu \mathrm{F}$$, the rms value of current in the circuit is ________ $$\mathrm{mA}$$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: IUPAC name of following hydrocarbon(X) is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Identify the incorrect statements about group 15 elements :

(A) Dinitrogen is a diatomic gas which acts like an inert gas at room temperature.

(B) The common oxidation states of these elements are $$-3,+3$$ and +5.

(C) Nitrogen has unique ability to form $$\mathrm{p} \pi-\mathrm{p} \pi$$ multiple bonds.

(D) The stability of +5 oxidation states increases down the group.

(E) Nitrogen shows a maximum covalency of 6.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement (I) : Kjeldahl method is applicable to estimate nitrogen in pyridine.

Statement (II) : The nitrogen present in pyridine can easily be converted into ammonium sulphate in Kjeldahl method.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
(C) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
(D) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In qualitative test for identification of presence of phosphorous, the compound is heated with an oxidising agent. Which is further treated with nitric acid and ammonium molybdate respectively. The yellow coloured precipitate obtained is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement (I) : Fusion of $$\mathrm{MnO}_2$$ with $$\mathrm{KOH}$$ and an oxidising agent gives dark green $$\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{MnO}_4$$.

Statement (II) : Manganate ion on electrolytic oxidation in alkaline medium gives permanganate ion.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(B) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
(C) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
(D) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement (I) : A Buffer solution is the mixture of a salt and an acid or a base mixed in any particular quantities

Statement (II) : Blood is naturally occurring buffer solution whose $$\mathrm{pH}$$ is maintained by $$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3 / \mathrm{HCO}_3{ }^{\ominus}$$ concentrations.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
(C) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(D) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement (I) : All the following compounds react with p-toluenesulfonyl chloride.

$$\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{NH}_2 \quad\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5\right)_2 \mathrm{NH} \quad\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5\right)_3 \mathrm{N}$$

Statement (II) : Their products in the above reaction are soluble is aqueous $$\mathrm{NaOH}$$.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
(C) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
(D) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II

LIST I
(Test)		 LIST II
(Identification)
A. Bayer's test I. Phenol
B. Ceric ammonium nitrate test II. Aldehyde
C. Phthalein dye test III. Alcoholic-OH group
D. Schiff's test IV. Unsaturation

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The shape of carbocation is :
options:
(A) tetrahedral
(B) diagonal pyramidal
(C) diagonal
(D) trigonal planar
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The equilibrium $$\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{CrO}_4^{2-}$$ is shifted to the right in :
options:
(A) a weakly acidic medium
(B) a basic medium
(C) a neutral medium
(D) an acidic medium
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which one the following compounds will readily react with dilute $$\mathrm{NaOH}$$ ?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Identify the correct statements about p-block elements and their compounds.

(A) Non metals have higher electronegativity than metals.

(B) Non metals have lower ionisation enthalpy than metals.

(C) Compounds formed between highly reactive nonmetals and highly reactive metals are generally ionic.

(D) The non-metal oxides are generally basic in nature.

(E) The metal oxides are generally acidic or neutral in nature.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A) (B) and (D) only
(B) (B) and (E) only
(C) (D) and (E) only
(D) (A) and (C) only
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For a reaction $$A \xrightarrow{\mathrm{K}_1} \mathrm{~B} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{K}_2} \mathrm{C}$$ If the rate of formation of B is set to be zero then the concentration of B is given by :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The correct sequence of acidic strength of the following aliphatic acids in their decreasing order is:

$$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{COOH}, \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}, \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{COOH}, \mathrm{HCOOH}$$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The emf of cell $$\mathrm{Tl}\left|\underset{(0.001 \mathrm{M})}{\mathrm{Tl}^{+}}\right| \underset{(0.01 \mathrm{M})}{\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}} \mid \mathrm{Cu}$$ is $$0.83 \mathrm{~V}$$ at $$298 \mathrm{~K}$$. It could be increased by :
options:
(A) increasing concentration of $$\mathrm{Tl}^{+}$$ ions
(B) increasing concentration of $$\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$$ ions
(C) increasing concentration of both $$\mathrm{Tl}^{+}$$ and $$\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$$ ions
(D) decreasing concentration of both $$\mathrm{Tl}^{+}$$ and $$\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$$ ions
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The reaction;

$$\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{H}_{2(\mathrm{~g})}+\mathrm{AgCl}_{(\mathrm{s})} \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{-}+\mathrm{Ag}_{(\mathrm{s})}$$

occurs in which of the following galvanic cell :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement (I) : $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$$ reactions are 'stereospecific', indicating that they result in the formation of only one stereo-isomer as the product.

Statement (II) : $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$$ reactions generally result in formation of product as racemic mixtures.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
(B) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(C) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
(D) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The number of optically active compounds from the following is _________.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Total number of aromatic compounds among the following compounds is ______.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: When $$\psi_{\mathrm{A}}$$ and $$\psi_{\mathrm{B}}$$ are the wave functions of atomic orbitals, then $$\sigma^*$$ is represented by :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Molality of an aqueous solution of urea is $$4.44 \mathrm{~m}$$. Mole fraction of urea in solution is $$x \times 10^{-3}$$, Value of $$x$$ is ________. (Integer answer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A solution is prepared by adding 1 mole ethyl alcohol in 9 mole water. The mass percent of solute in the solution is ________ (Integer answer) (Given : Molar mass in $$\mathrm{g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$ Ethyl alcohol : 46 water : 18)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Wavenumber for a radiation having 5800 $$\mathop A\limits^o $$ wavelength is $$x \times 10 \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$$ The value of $$x$$ is ________. (Integer answer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Two moles of benzaldehyde and one mole of acetone under alkaline conditions using aqueous $$\mathrm{NaOH}$$ after heating gives $$x$$ as the major product. The number of $$\pi$$ bonds in the product $$x$$ is ______.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Number of molecules having bond order 2 from the following molecules is _________.

$$\mathrm{C}_2, \mathrm{O}_2, \mathrm{Be}_2, \mathrm{Li}_2, \mathrm{Ne}_2, \mathrm{~N}_2, \mathrm{He}_2$$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The total number of carbon atoms present in tyrosine, an amino acid, is ________.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Total number of unpaired electrons in the complex ions $$[\mathrm{Co}(\mathrm{NH}_3)_6]^{3+}$$ and $$[\mathrm{NiCl}_4]^{2-}$$ is ________.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: $$\Delta_{\text {vap }} \mathrm{H}^{\ominus}$$ for water is $$+40.79 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$ at 1 bar and $$100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$. Change in internal energy for this vapourisation under same condition is ________ $$\mathrm{kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$. (Integer answer) (Given $$\mathrm{R}=8.3 \mathrm{~JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$)
questions.subject: Chemistry
